feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to Retrieve Data from HANA DB Using CAP for OData Service in SAP Business Application Studio?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working on a project where I need to retrieve data from an SAP HANA database using an OData service. I plan to use an OData adapter in SAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration) for this purpose. From my understanding, I need to create a CAP (Cloud Application Programming) project in SAP Business Application Studio to expose the data via an OData service.

Could someone guide me through the steps to achieve this? Additionally, if there are any comprehensive documentation or tutorials available, please share them.

Thank you in advance for your assistance!

Best regards,

Anildeep

SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database SAP HANA service for SAP BTP SAP Business Application Studio Cloud Integration SAP Integration Suite SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

entry.title: Guidance Needed: Replicating Employee Data from Workday to SuccessFactors Employee Central Using SA
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am new to SuccessFactors integration and need some guidance on a project where we must integrate employee data into SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC). Specifically, we need to handle files (Terminations.csv, NewHires.csv, and Changes.csv) from the source system and upsert this data into SuccessFactors EC. The processing must occur in sequence to ensure data integrity, and we need to skip processing of files if the data already exists in SuccessFactors EC.

Given that Workday is not within our project scope, our focus is on using SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) for file conversion and data handling.

If anyone has experience with replicating employee master data from Workday to SuccessFactors EC using SAP CPI, I would greatly appreciate your insights on the following:

  1. iFlow Implementation:

    • How to design and configure iFlows to process files (Terminations.csv, NewHires.csv, Changes.csv) in the correct sequence.
    • Best practices for setting up and configuring file adapters, content modifiers, and SuccessFactors EC adapters.

  2. Data Transformation and Upsertion:

    • Detailed steps for transforming and mapping data from the source files to the SuccessFactors EC format.
    • How to handle upserting new hires, applying changes, and processing terminations, including how to skip files if the data already exists.

  3. Error Handling and Monitoring:

    • Recommended strategies for managing errors and exceptions during file processing.
    • Tips for effective monitoring and ensuring successful execution of the integration flows.

Your detailed guidance and examples of handling similar scenarios with SuccessFactors EC inbound integrations would be extremely valuable.

entry.title: Set AS2 Filename in receiver channel - BTP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement to post the files to partner using AS2 adapter along with the file extension. The channel configurations are supporting only filename & timestamp in B2B TPM. Did anyone came across this scenario, please help me with the solution.

For example: The file name should be EDI_20240913.txt

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: SAP CPI Production Order Creation Fails Due to Invalid Time Format in OData Response
entry.content:
Hi team,

I'm working on a SAP CPI iFlow that creates production orders in SAP S4H, using the Production Order (Version 2) API.

I started by testing the connection between CPI and SAP, so I only have a Content Modifier with the payload and a Request Reply to connect to the OData adapter.

The payload is as follows:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<A_ProductionOrder_2>
<A_ProductionOrder_2Type>
<ManufacturingOrderType>ZP01</ManufacturingOrderType>
<Material>SG002</Material>
<ProductionPlant>3516</ProductionPlant>
<MRPController>001</MRPController>
<MfgOrderPlannedStartDate>2024-09-15T00:00:00</MfgOrderPlannedStartDate>
<MfgOrderPlannedStartTime>00:00:00</MfgOrderPlannedStartTime>
<MfgOrderPlannedEndDate>2024-09-16T00:00:00</MfgOrderPlannedEndDate>
<MfgOrderPlannedEndTime>00:00:00</MfgOrderPlannedEndTime>
<ProductionUnit>PC</ProductionUnit>
<TotalQuantity>10</TotalQuantity>
</A_ProductionOrder_2Type>
</A_ProductionOrder_2>

With this payload, I'm successfully creating production orders, so it doesn't seem like the payload is incorrect. However, CPI is always returning the following error:

com.sap.gateway.core.ip.processor.exception.ODataProcessingException: com.sap.gateway.core.ip.processor.exception.ODataProcessingException: Exception occurred in converting [class java.util.GregorianCalendar] with value [java.util.GregorianCalendar[time=?,areFieldsSet=false,areAllFieldsSet=false,lenient=false,zone=sun.util.calendar.ZoneInfo[id="Etc/UTC",offset=0,dstSavings=0,useDaylight=false,transitions=0,lastRule=null],firstDayOfWeek=1,minimalDaysInFirstWeek=1,ERA=?,YEAR=?,MONTH=?,WEEK_OF_YEAR=?,WEEK_OF_MONTH=?,DAY_OF_MONTH=?,DAY_OF_YEAR=?,DAY_OF_WEEK=?,DAY_OF_WEEK_IN_MONTH=?,AM_PM=?,HOUR=?,HOUR_OF_DAY=24,MINUTE=0,SECOND=0,MILLISECOND=?,ZONE_OFFSET=?,DST_OFFSET=?]] to String., cause: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: HOUR_OF_DAY

I believe the issue is related to two tags in the Request Reply response: MfgOrderPlannedEndTime and MfgOrderScheduledEndTime, with the value PT24H00M00S. It seems that java.util.GregorianCalendar only accepts hours in the range of 0-23, but according to SAP documentation, the hour 24:00 is allowed (which seems incorrect to me).

I'm struggling to find a solution for this issue, as it seems to be a very basic error.

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: SAP BTP Integration Suite artifact upload issue using CI/CD pipeline
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am trying to upload the integration artifacts from Github using SAP Integration Suite Artifacts CICD pipeline, however I am getting the below error in ‘Upload’ step.

The unzipped artifact folder with Manifest file are already there in the Github repository, not sure how to resolve this.

“ hudson.AbortException: [integrationArtifactUpload] Step execution failed (category: undefined). Error: HTTP POST request to https://<trialcpiaccount>/api/v1/IntegrationDesigntimeArtifacts failed with error: <?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?><error xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata"><code>Bad Request</code><message xml:lang="en">Integration flow project must contain a manifest file.</message></error> “

Folder structure in Github : 

 

Would appreciate if anybody could help.

Thanks

entry.title: Publishing API in APIGEE with OData backend URL
entry.content:
Experts,

In our environment, have many open-source applications that are integrated through APIGEE for internal and external data integration through Restful APIs. We are an SAP ECC application and would like to publish an API in APIGEE using OData as backend. any expert's advice from their previous experience with similar task is greatly appreciated....

entry.title: Do we have the relevant S4 HANA standard V2/V4 API for the BAPI "BAPI_ACC_ACTIVITY_ALLOC_POST"?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

As part of clean core methodology we need to implement the similar call of posting the activity hours on WBS timesheet in S4 from CPI. A similar BAPI "BAPI_ACC_ACTIVITY_ALLOC_POST" is there but wanted to know do we have the relevant S4 HANA standard V2/V4 API for similar BAPI functionality?

Thanks,

Ahmed

entry.title: Combining Responses from HTTP adapter in CPI
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have created a flow where I am posting each record in S/4 and getting a document number against each successful posting. If the source file having 10 records, then I have to capture 10 document numbers from the responses and combine them and log as a text file.

Updated with 2 records and the response text are coming as below. Nedd to combine 2 & 3 body with newline and log that as text file

 

Please suggest how can this be achieved.

entry.title: Read SFTP Sender Excel file sheetname in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a scenario of getting an XLSX file from an SFTP (sender) using SAP CPI and sending to SAP S4. Here I need to get the sheet name before conversion to xml but I don't know how to do this reading to an Excel file.

Kindly share the groovy script code to read this before using converter class to convert to xml.

entry.title: How to get data sent from Gmail in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Since SAP Integration Suite can use IMAP and POP3 protocols, how can I use these protocols to receive emails in CPI?
What are the requirements for the sender side? If you have any experience with this, please respond.

entry.title: CDN Integration with Accelerator based storefront
entry.content:
Wherever there is any redirection triggered from the the Commerce pods, we observed that HttpServletRequestWrapper.getServerName() does not return the expected Akamai domain but instead returns the internal server name that corresponds to the storefront domain url defined in the cloud portal. 

Can you please provide some guidance on how we can set up HttpServletRequestWrapper.getServerName() to return the CDN domain?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: AIF monitoring in SAP Integration Suite(Cloud Integration)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are currently using SAP PO/PI 7.5 as Integration platform with in our landscape..

To do AIF(application interface framework) monitoring, to see the end to end message flow from/to S4/ECC regarding outbound/inbound interfaces we have customized Fiori page...

we are using the Fiori page for the following cases..

(i) AIF Messages (ii) AIF Monitoring (iii) Interface monitoring...

In that Fiori page, it shows status of the messages on SAP backend & SAP PO/PI.. also if someone clicks on SAP PO status "hyperlink" of that specific message then it re-directs to the SAP PO monitoring page of that specific message... it is possible to link SAP backend and SAP PO messsage on Fiori page because message ID for outbound messages is same on SAP backend & SAP PO/PI.. it is easier to fetch SAP PO status of the specific message...

Fiori page with SAP PO Message status Hyperlink (here "Success" is hyperlink)..

SAP PO monitoring page of that specific message

We are planning to migrate all the interfaces from SAP PO/PI to SAP Integration Suite(Cloud Integration)..

Now, what are different alternate options available within SAP BTP to cater this requirement..

1. BTP Based Fiori app 

2. SAP Cloud ALM for end to end monitoring..

AFAIK, the option 1) above is expensive since Fiori developers has to put of lot of efforts to build such app for AIF monitoring.. Could option 2) completely replace the above solution implemented in SAP PO/PI?? Could you please let me know which is better option in terms various factors like Cost, efforts, time to develope/implement, SAP recommendation, standard solution etc.., Thank you!

//Ramu

entry.title: AMQP sender flow deployment issue: POLL_FAILED
entry.content:
Hi All,
we are getting below error when deploying a AMQP based flow:

the flow deployed successfully after un-deploying other AMQP based flow.
are there any constraint issues here? 

Below is the Resource Monitor page for reference:

 



SAP Event Mesh Cloud Integration

entry.title: How can I find the status of a running iFlow by an external client e.g. a Powershell script
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I am trying to trigger an iFlow with an HTTP endpoint from a Powershell script. However it is a long running flow and I would like to have an option to monitor that from the script itself periodically. 

is there a monitoring API available that can do that? 

Thanks,

Prabal

entry.title: How could we configure the ServiceNow Open Connector using JWT authentication?
entry.content:
How could we configure the ServiceNow Open Connector using JWT authentication? By default, the authentication is OAuth 2.0. We need some clarification on this. 

Thank you in advance,

BR

entry.title: What's the best way to monitor the progress of an iFlow using a PowerShell script?
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I am attempting to trigger an iFlow using an HTTP endpoint from a PowerShell script. Since it is a long-running flow, I would like to incorporate a periodic monitoring feature within the script itself.

Is there a monitoring API available that can facilitate this?

Thanks,

Himanshu

entry.title: Dynamic Extension Setting for File Name Output in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am now trying doing file decompression (Unzip) within SAP CPi. I unzip two files (.txt and .csv) contained in one ZIP file and store them on an FTP server using the FTP Adapter.

In the FTP Adapter settings, there's a place to set the name of the output files (1 txt file and 1 csv file), but how can I dynamically set the "extension" in this case?

 

As an example, this is what I expect.

Input Files' (to be unzipped) Name

> AAA.zip (which contains BBB.txt and CCC.csv)

Output Files

>BBB.txt and CCC.csv

(By the way, when I left the file name setting of FTP adapter blank, then the output files came out as BBB and CCC, which means there was no extension.)

(The attached screenshot shows where the file name is set.)

Thank you.

entry.title: How to increase timeout in powershell script while invoking BTP iflow using Invoke-WebRequest?
entry.content:
Hi All,
I am using PowerShell script Invoke-WebRequest command to trigger an SAP BTP iFlow. When the iflow processes quickly (less than 2 minutes), the PowerShell script receives the correct status. However, if the iflow takes longer than 2 minutes, even though you've set a timeout of 1 hour, the script fails with a "Termination Error (Invoke-webRequest): The connection closed Unexpectedly" message

Thanks 
Himanshu

entry.title: How to get the actual attachment name from Outbound proxy to CPI
entry.content:
Hi Everyone ,
I am trying to capture the attachment names in SAP CPI, from an outbound proxy call from SAP- but I am only getting the attachment ID as name and not the actual attachment name.
I am using the below line in my Groovy - message.setProperty("attachmentName",attachment.getName());
where attachment is a DataHandler object.
In proxy, I have the below name of the attachments -

but in CPI I am getting the attachment name as -payload-000D3A03E1FD1EDF9FC56D07DA1F65E6@sap.com

Could you please help me with this to get the actual attachment name?

Thanks

Sugata

entry.title: Size Limit For Exchange Property in Content Modifier in SAP CPI?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am curious about if there's any size limit for Exchange Property in Content Modifier in SAP CPI?

To be specific, to loop-getting data, I use Looping Process Call to get payload, and create a property (by using content modifier) in order to temporarily store and append data being fetched. The configuration of the property is as follow.

Action: Create

Name: StorePayload

Source Type: Expression

Source Value: ${property.StorePayload}${in.body}

 

I wonder how much data can I temporarily store in this property. (50MB? 100MB?)

 

Thank you in advance.
